author: vouser123
text: Fixed on PR #326 / branch codex/pt-yj7-nextjs. /program now resolves patient-scoped dosage context from users data before bootstrap and replay, uses the resolved patient users.id for dosage writes, and refreshes the offline queue against that same context on reconnect. Chrome DevTools verification on the Vercel preview: signed in as test therapist, confirmed the dosage banner showed Test Patient, queued an offline dosage create for Long Arc Quad (2 x 11), reconnected, and observed the queue clear without the prior 403 Patient does not belong to this therapist / 404 Program not found loop.

author: vouser123
text: Fixed on PR #326 / branch codex/pt-yj7-nextjs. /program now uses the shared Toast component plus useToast hook instead of page-local toast state and inline toast markup. The active docs were updated in the same change to document the shared pattern and the patient-context rule for patient-scoped routes.

author: vouser123
text: 2026-03-20 preview verification on PR #326: therapist dosage replay regression is resolved. In Chrome DevTools on https://pt-rehab-git-codex-pt-yj7-nextjs-pt-tracker.vercel.app/program, signed in as the test therapist and confirmed /program showed Test Patient as the dosage context. While offline, created a Long Arc Quad dosage of 2 x 11 and saw the queued change update locally. After reconnect, the queue cleared and the dosage remained present without the earlier 403/404 replay failure. Additional check: signed in as the test patient in a separate isolated browser context and confirmed the same page showed Test Patient as the dosage context there as well. Remaining gap: offline navigation to /program still fails at the page-shell level with ERR_INTERNET_DISCONNECTED, which remains the separate service-worker/root-cache blocker already tracked under pt-41a rather than a dosage replay issue.

author: vouser123
text: In-flight refactor structure as of 2026-03-20 night session: /program is being slimmed toward the same page-as-orchestrator pattern used by /index. New component split underway: components/ProgramRolesWorkspace.js owns the roles workspace shell (section heading, search/select controls, hands off add/remove UI to existing ProgramRolesSection); components/ProgramDosageWorkspace.js owns the dosage workspace shell (section heading, patient-context banner, search/select controls, summary card, edit/set button). New hook split underway: hooks/useProgramVocabActions.js owns optimistic vocabulary create/update/delete handlers so hooks/useProgramMutationActions.js can shrink back to exercise save + role + dosage concerns only. Goal state: pages/program.js stays responsible for route auth/bootstrap/orchestration, modal open state, and wiring shared hooks/components together rather than owning full workspace markup.

author: vouser123
text: Related structural follow-up while offline queue work is still active: size-cap remediation is being handled under pt-roz. The intended /program ownership after the split is: page = route orchestration/bootstrap; ProgramRolesWorkspace = roles workspace shell; ProgramDosageWorkspace = dosage workspace shell; useProgramMutationActions = exercise/role/dosage optimistic writes; useProgramVocabActions = vocabulary optimistic writes; useProgramOfflineQueue = queue lifecycle/replay. This note is to preserve the target shape in case the current session is interrupted before the refactor lands.

author: vouser123
text: 2026-03-21 completion update: /program now follows the page-as-orchestrator pattern more closely. The page keeps auth/bootstrap/queue/modal orchestration while selector and workspace UI moved into shared components: ProgramExerciseSelector, ExerciseRolesWorkspace, and ProgramDosageWorkspace. Vocabulary mutations moved into hooks/useProgramVocabActions.js so hooks/useProgramMutationActions.js now owns exercise save + role + dosage writes only. Because legacy behavior hosted the editor and roles surfaces from more than one route, the roles workspace was renamed to ExerciseRolesWorkspace and deferred follow-up beads were created for future host wiring: pt-e5a (rehab coverage roles host) and pt-ve3 (tracker exercise-editor host). Validation completed: npm run build passed.

author: vouser123
text: 2026-03-21 night wrap-up: reopened the queue container after the tracker cleanup because the intended use is a fresh next-session pointer for the current agent lane, not a permanent backlog. Current Codex recommendation is to resume in the tracker offline verification lane next session: pt-rb8, pt-41a, and pt-zb3.
